title: Self-Knowledge Signals
summary: Self-knowledge signals follow the tools, practices, and public conversations that make behaviour more visible.

items.name: Journaling tools are getting smarter
items.summary: Reflection apps now use prompts and summaries.
items.whyItMatters: Software is becoming part of introspection.

items.name: Wearables are making state visible
items.summary: Sleep, stress, and activity data are easier to track.
items.whyItMatters: People can now tie feelings to objective signals.

items.name: Health trackers
items.summary: Wearables help people see patterns in routine.

items.name: Psychology research centres
items.summary: Evidence keeps refining what self-awareness means.
items.whyItMatters: Scientific findings protect against pure self-help myth.

items.name: Mindfulness institutions
items.whyItMatters: Practice and reflection are being formalised.

items.name: James Clear
items.whyItMatters: His work keeps focus on systems, not just intention.

items.name: Daniel Kahneman
items.whyItMatters: He shows why introspection needs evidence.

items.name: Can self-knowledge be quantified?
items.summary: Data can help, but some things stay qualitative.
items.whyItMatters: The debate shapes how people use apps and coaching.

items.name: Is self-optimisation helping or harming?
items.summary: Some think it clarifies life; others think it creates anxiety.
items.whyItMatters: This tension is central to modern self-improvement culture.
